channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Is 007 First Light Canon In James Bond? 007 First Light Vs Bond Movies, Explained
item.content:encoded:
James Bond has one of the richest histories out there, with the character dating all the way back to the 1950s. Almost a decade later, Sean Connery undertook the role of the super spy in Dr. No, and 007 has been a cinema staple ever since.

His dominance didn’t stop at the big screen, as he’s had a ton of video game spin-offs, with 007 First Light being the latest. As you can imagine, it can be tough to keep up with the Bond canon, and IO Interactive’s upcoming game has thrown another spanner in the works with their brand new origin story.

It begs the question: is 007 First Light part of the James Bond canon, and is the game related to the films in any way? For all you Bond purists out there, hopefully this clears the air a little bit.

What Is The James Bond Canon?

Generally speaking, there have been two canon runs for James Bond, with the first 20 films all part of the same canon, whilst Daniel Craig’s movies served as a reboot for the franchise.

Whilst it might seem hard to wrap your head around, since there were five different Bond actors throughout the original film franchise, it’s best to think of them like a comic book. They all loosely tied into one another, but with different writers and artists throughout the years, telling one winding adventure.

  • More Insider Gaming: 007 First Light Cast Revealed—All Voice Actors, Explained

However, when Daniel Craig took the hot seat with Casino Royale in 2006, it served as a reboot for the Bond franchise, resetting the canon. All of Craig’s films are much stricter with continuity, as they flow seamlessly from one to the next.

Of course, now that it’s wrapped up, it’s unclear whether a new actor will step in and continue his story, or if we’ll see another Bond reboot. Pair that with 007 First Light coming out, and you can see where the confusion stems from: will the game fit into a prior canon, or could it maybe be the first step to succeeding Daniel Craig?

Is 007 First Light Canon In James Bond?

The simple answer is no, 007 First Light isn’t canon in the general James Bond lore, as the game doesn’t tie into the movies in any way.

Instead, IO Interactive is looking to tell its own unique James Bond story, with the game serving as an origin story. You’ll follow Bond as he begins as an MI6 recruit and eventually evolves into the 007 agent we all know him for.

  • More Insider Gaming: All 007 First Light Pre-Order Bonuses, Editions & Prices, Explained

Naturally, the developers have taken tons of inspiration from the plethora of source material, so in spirit it should feel like a story ripped straight from the classic Bond stories that you’ve grown to love—just adapted to a video game format.

Interestingly, it was originally discussed whether the game would be canonical when IOI had early talks with Amazon MGM, the owners of the Bond IP. Despite that, it was ultimately decided that the game would live alongside the future of 007, but not be overtly connected.

Were you hoping 007 First Light would be canonical to other James Bond entries? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more 007 First Light content, check out the following:

  • 007 First Light Game Pass & Early Access Status, Explained
  • 007 First Light Release Date & All Platforms, Explained
  • How Long Is 007 First Light? Game Length & Total Hours, Explained

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Subnautica 2 Early Access PC System Requirements—How Much Space Does Subnautica 2 Early Access Need?
item.content:encoded:
The ocean is about to be the biggest and best it’s ever been in a Subnautica game. New features are likely to require more power, though, so find out the minimum, recommended, ultra, and ultra++ PC specs and requirements in Subnautica 2 early access.

Yes, Subnautica 2 early access is also on Xbox, but I imagine the majority of players will be on PC. Previous games haven’t been too bad in terms of PC specs and requirements, but it’s a whole other kettle of ocean-living fish for Subnautica 2 EA.

Subnautica 2 Early Access Minimum Requirements on PC

Here’s the complete list of Subnautica 2 early access minimum PC specs:

  • GPU: Nvidia GTX 1660 / AMD RX 5500XT
  • CPU: Intel i5-8400 / AMD Ryzen 5 2600
  • OS: Windows 10/11
  • Memory: 12 GB
  • GPU Memory: 6 GB
  • Resolution & Preset: 1080p / Low
  • FPS: 30 FPS

Subnautica 2 Early Access Recommended Requirements on PC

Now it’s time for the recommended requirements on PC, which should serve as a baseline for most players:

  • GPU: Nvidia RTX 3070 / AMD RX 6700XT
  • CPU: Intel i7-13700 / AMD Ryzen 7 7700X
  • OS: Windows 11
  • Memory: 16 GB
  • GPU Memory: 8 GB
  • Resolution & Preset: 1440p / Medium
  • FPS: 60 FPS

Subnautica 2 Early Access Ultra Requirements on PC

Let’s step it up a notch now with Subnautica 2 early access Ultra requirements; asking more of your PC.

  • GPU: Nvidia RTX 4070 / AMD RX 6900XT
  • CPU: Intel i7-13700 / AMD Ryzen 7 7700X
  • OS: Windows 11
  • Memory: 32 GB
  • GPU Memory: 12 GB
  • Resolution & Preset: 1440p / High
  • FPS: 60 FPS

Subnautica 2 Early Access Ultra++ Requirements on PC

Now for the final boss: The Subnautica 2 early access Ultra++ PC specs for the cleanest deep-sea exploration:

  • GPU: Nvidia RTX 5070TI / AMD RX 7900XTX
  • CPU: Intel i9-14900 / AMD Ryzen 9 7900X3D
  • OS: Windows 11
  • Memory: 32 GB
  • GPU Memory: 16 GB
  • Resolution & Preset: 4K / High
  • FPS: 60 FPS

Subnautica 2 Early Access Storage Space Required

According to Steam, you need at least 50 GB of available space for Subnautica 2 early access.

To make room for Subnautica 2 early access and subsequent updates, you’ll need to free up 50 GB of storage space, if you haven’t already.

What are your first impressions of the Subnautica 2 system requirements, then? Too punishing for early access? Or, if your computer is a beast, feel free to let the Insider Gaming Discord community know about it!

We have more Subnautica 2 early access guides for you to check out:

  • Does Subnautica 2 Early Access Have Co-op or Multiplayer?
  • Subnautica 2 Early Access Xbox Game Pass Status, Explained
  • How Long Will Subnautica 2 Be In Early Access?
  • How Much Does Subnautica 2 Early Access Cost?
  • Subnautica 2 Early Access Release Date, Times & All Platforms

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Dying Light Franchise Director Steps Down After 13 Years at Techland
item.content:encoded:
Dying Light’s leadership team has undergone a drastic change today as Tymon Smektala, veteran developer, announces his departure from Techland after thirteen years at the firm. Smektala started with Techland in May 2013, working as a game designer, before moving into a production role on the first Dying Light game.

Smektala departs the company, having most recently served almost four years as the Franchise Director behind the entire Dying Light series, the last deliverable of which role was to push out Dying Light: The Beast.

Tymon Smektala Departs Techland After a Decade

In a post on social media, Tymon Smektala, former Franchise Director of Dying Light at Techland, wrote:

After years with Dying Light, I’m moving into a new chapter. It’s been an incredible journey, and I’m proud of what we’ve built.

Thank you to all the talented people of Techland I’ve had the chance to work with – across both development and publishing – for the passion, energy, and shared ambition to make Dying Light the ultimate zombie experience. We made it happen, together!

And to our amazing community: your love, engagement, and vocal support have always been our North Star. You helped shape what Dying Light became.

The franchise is in great hands, and I’m excited to see it grow even further, and I can’t wait to experience its next chapter as a player. I’ll always root for Dying Light!

Good night, and good luck!

Last year, Techland released Dying Light: The Beast, a whirlwind title that brought Kyle Crane, the game’s first protagonist, back into the limelight. It effectively kickstarted a new chapter of the Dying Light saga, which is what I learned when I sat down with Tymon a year ago in Los Angeles.

That was when he told me that Dying Light: The Beast was being thought of internally as Dying Light 3.

It was also when I learned more about the nugget of a game that was Dying Light: Hellrunners, a title that ultimately never saw the light of day.

Smektala hasn’t indicated what he moves onto, but I’m excited to see where he goes next.

Are you surprised to learn about this big industry move? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Build a Rocket Boy has made more layoffs

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Does Subnautica 2 Early Access Have Co-op or Multiplayer? Single-Player Status, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Previous Subnautica adventures have been compelling journeys under the sea. One thing that could have made them even more enjoyable (and less terrifying) is getting to explore the water with someone else in co-op multiplayer.

Games have to evolve, right? If you stick to the same time formula for too long without much innovation, then it might have been best not to release something new. Subnautica 2 early access delivers a sprinkling of something new in almost every way, but the addition of any multiplayer would be a game-changer.

Does Subnautica 2 Early Access Have Multiplayer or Co-op?

It’s happening: Subnautica 2 early access features fully-implemented multiplayer AND co-op functionality, allowing up to four people to join you in your travels.

  • More Insider Gaming: Subnautica 2 Early Access Xbox Game Pass Status, Explained

The news many of you have been waiting for in a Subnautica sequel. Here’s the confirmation ripped straight from the Subnautica 2 Discord FAQ:

‘Yes. Subnautica 2 is being designed as a single-player experience that you can optionally play with friends, in up to 4-player co-op.’

You only have to look at the screenshot above to see it in action. Complete four-player online co-op and multiplayer for you to enjoy with your friends. You can add all the new submersibles and fish you want, give the people multiplayer, and they’ll be happy no matter how long Subnautica 2 is in early access.

Can You Play Subnautica 2 Early Access Single-Player?

Subnautica 2 can also be enjoyed as a single-player game, like the previous two titles, if you want to immerse yourself in a solo world.

Just to reiterate, as it’s made clear in the earlier FAQ statement, Subnautica 2 isn’t abandoning its single-player focus. Multiplayer and co-op play are optional, and you’re free to explore the world at your own leisure.

  • More Insider Gaming: Subnautica 2 Early Access Release Date, Times & All Platforms

If you have any doubts about the game’s intent, even the Steam page explains: ‘Subnautica 2 is being crafted as a single-player experience.’

This is the focus, so don’t be worried about multiplayer taking over.

The lawsuit allegations haven’t helped Subnautica 2, but they shouldn’t take away from what is going to be a memorable sequel. Whether you’re excited about the game in general, or specifically the addition of multiplayer, why not talk about Subnautica 2 through our Insider Gaming Discord?

Don’t forget to check out how much Subnautica 2 early access will cost.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: 007 First Light Nintendo Switch 2 Release Could be Delayed Again
item.content:encoded:
The IO Interactive CEO has revealed that the 007 First Light Switch 2 version’s release could be delayed further, but it is coming for sure.

007 First Light’s Switch 2 port was first delayed indefinitely, and then IO Interactive revealed that it would arrive later this summer. However, it now seems the game will be delayed further so the developers can provide the best possible experience for players on Nintendo’s platform.

007 First Light Is Already Running on the Switch 2

While talking to The Game Business, IO Interactive’s CEO has addressed a possible second delay for the 007 First Light Switch 2 version. When asked about it’s status, Hakan Abrak, stated that the game is already running on the Switch 2, however, they don’t plan to release it until they “make it as good as it can be”. The CEO is not willing to hear criticisms of this variant, such as players stating that “it wasn’t a good version.”

Abrak added, that they “need a bit more time to get it where we want it to be.” The developers had first stated Summer 2026, but it seems that it will be pushed to late summer. However, the CEO is confident that the Switch 2 version will come. Abrak concluded, Bond has a special place on Nintendo. And I promise I will do everything I can, and I can do quite a lot being the CEO, to get that in a great shape.”

In other news, the CEO has also stated that a sequel for the 007 First Light franchise depends on public reception. Additionally, IO Interactive’s CEO has confirmed that their new online fantasy RPG IP, Project Fantasy, is in a safe place. What are your thoughts on 007 First Light’s Switch 2 release being delayed further? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about MindsEye developers laying off more staff post DLC launch. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: IO Interactive’s Project Fantasy Is In a “Very Healthy Space”
item.content:encoded:
IO Interactive’s CEO has informed players that Project Fantasy is still in development and remains financed.

Project Fantasy is a new IP in development at IO Interactive. It is an online fantasy RPG that features a world and a game built from the ground up to entertain players and expand for many years to come. There were rumors that the game might have lost its finances, but that doesn’t seem to be the case.

IO Interactive CEO Didn’t Reveal the Partner for Project Fantasy, But Confirms It’s Safe

In a new interview with The Game Business, IO Interactive CEO Hakan Abrak briefly discussed Project Fantasy’s status. Abrak first stated that he won’t reveal the partner and will not confirm or deny whether Xbox is involved in the project. However, what he could confirm was that “Project Fantasy is in a very healthy place, it’s still financed, and it’s amazing.” So any players who were waiting for this project can rest easy.

For those wondering about the game, we don’t know many details, but the developers have said it feels familiar, yet IOI is also on a journey unlike any we have been on before. The developers have also stated how they ended up exploring this genre:

For many, this journey is also a deeply personal one that began long before we started making games for a living – and it started with the fantasy genre. From the “Fighting Fantasy” books, compelling you to choose your path, alone, against wizards, lizards, and thief kings. To the togetherness, camaraderie, agony, and delight found around the tabletop. For some, it meant taking the role of a game master: Part storyteller, part AI opponent, part guide, part villain. For everyone around that table, it meant creativity, imagination, building a world together, and a shared goal in creating a great game experience.

In other news, IO Interactive and Build a Rocket Boy’s publishing collaboration for MindsEye has concluded. Additionally, IO Interactive CEO Hakan Abrak spoke about the future of the 007 franchise and whether a sequel is on the cards. What are your thoughts on Project Fantasy being in a “very healthy space”? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the new Worms game being leaked in a bizarre way. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
